rationale: The two papers share a central construct—consumer response to user/customer-ideated versus designer-designed products—and the cited paper provides core empirical evidence (randomized field experiments on design-source labeling effects and quality inference). The citing note explicitly says it addresses mixed prior evidence on nonparticipating consumers’ preference and extends prior user-design research with a new moderator/mechanisms, which makes this citation plausibly foundational or directly comparative rather than incidental.
